page.title: 12 Sectors of Mexican Federal Procurement
page.subtitle: {{totalValue}} across {{years}} years of COMPRANET data
page.subtitleFallback: ~9.9T MXN across 23 years of COMPRANET data
page.rankedBy: Ranked by avg risk score — click to inspect
page.modelNote: Agriculture's elevated risk rate reflects structural market concentration (93% direct awards) and training set composition (Segalmex/DICONSA/LICONSA cases), not necessarily higher corruption prevalence. Health sector carries larger value-weighted risk exposure. Labor (Trabajo) ranks #1 in avg risk due to its small size (48K contracts): GT-labeled vendors from other sectors (DICONSA, Edenred, Toka, AXTEL) hold 31% of its spend, inflating sector averages — not evidence of elevated corruption in labor institutions.

heatmap.title: Sector Risk Heatmap
heatmap.description: Color encodes relative risk ranking per metric — red is highest risk, green is lowest. Click a row to open that sector's full profile.

table.title: Sector Comparison
table.subtitle: Click column headers to sort. Click a sector name to view its full profile.

diagnostico.title: The National Diagnostic
diagnostico.subtitleDynamic: Systemic risk analysis by sector in federal public procurement. {{count}} sectors under surveillance. Results based on {{contracts}} contracts.
diagnostico.subtitleFallback: Systemic risk analysis by sector in federal public procurement. 12 sectors under surveillance. Results based on 3.05 million contracts.
diagnostico.hallazgoText: The Health sector carries the highest absolute risk: ghost-company networks in IMSS, ISSSTE, and COVID-19 emergency purchases represent over $200B MXN in high-risk contracts.
diagnostico.sourceFootnote: Source: COMPRANET 2002–2025, risk model v6.5 · Values normalized min-max per metric

editorial.headline: Where risk concentrates in federal spending
editorial.body: 12 sectors. 3.06 million contracts. MXN 9.88 trillion. The v6.5 model assigns risk scores to each contract using 9 statistical indicators calibrated against 748 documented corruption cases. Agriculture leads: 93.5% direct awards, 0.369 average risk.

elevatedRisk.title: Elevated High-Risk Rate
elevatedRisk.body: {{rate}}% high-risk rate is significantly above the platform average of {{baseline}}%.

lowSignal.body: {{rate}}% high-risk rate is unusually low — may reflect data quality gaps or structural sector characteristics.

highDA.body: {{rate}}% of contracts are direct awards, limiting competitive transparency.

singleBid.title: Single-Bid Procedures
singleBid.body: {{rate}}% of competitive procedures had only one bidder.

factorDesc.single_bid: Competitive procedure received only one offer
factorDesc.price_anomaly: Contract value is 3x above sector median
factorDesc.short_ad_period: Advertisement window too short for real competition
factorDesc.short_ad_lt5d: Under 5 days between publication and award
factorDesc.short_ad_lt15d: Under 15 days between publication and award
factorDesc.short_ad_lt30d: Under 30 days between publication and award
factorDesc.vendor_concentration: One vendor controls >30% of sector contracts
factorDesc.threshold_splitting: Multiple same-day contracts to avoid oversight thresholds
factorDesc.network_risk: Vendor belongs to a connected group of related companies
factorDesc.industry_mismatch: Vendor's primary industry doesn't match contract scope
factorDesc.co_bid_high: Vendor co-bids in >80% of procedures with a partner
factorDesc.co_bid_med: Vendor co-bids in 50–80% of procedures with a partner
factorDesc.price_hyp: Statistical outlier flagged by IQR price model
